original_abstract:
The concept of the "Smart Classroom" has evolved significantly, moving beyond basic digitalization to reflect the sophisticated integration of technology into modern educational spaces. Driven by rapid technological progress and the increasing demand for flexible environments that support both in-person and remote learning, Artificial Intelligence (AI) and emerging smart technologies have become central to the classroom experience. This paper explores the intersection of AI and education through a comprehensive literature review of smart classroom developments.

Our survey categorizes key technologies into three primary pillars: effective classroom management to optimize environmental comfort and safety, the application of smart teaching aids to foster engagement, and the implementation of automated systems for student performance assessment. Beyond reviewing specific technological milestones in these areas, we analyze the underlying role of AI, providing a clear understanding of its necessity in driving these innovations. Furthermore, we employ a SWOT analysis to critically evaluate the strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats associated with adopting AI in educational settings, while addressing future challenges and research directions. This review serves as a roadmap for educators to understand the practical potential and limitations of AI, and for AI professionals to gain insight into the unique pedagogical requirements and technical complexities of educational systems.
humanized_abstract:
A Smart Classroom refers to a class-room that not only achieves the digitalization but also undergoes significant transformation in terms of technology and that enhances the class-room environment. With the faster evolution of technology and need for flexible teaching and learning, based on Artificial Intelligence (AI) and other emerging smart technologies the Smart Classroom has transformed into a far more interactive place. This paper examines the concept of Smart Classroom using a thorough review of the literature concerning AI in education.

The key technologies are divided into three aspects: (1) the classroom environment management including the thermal and air quality comfort and the safety aspect, (2) smart learning resources, and (3) the automatic evaluation system for students’ learning progress. In addition to looking into the progress of various technological developments, we will discuss the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the context of education, and elaborate on the SWOT analysis for integrating AI in the education sector. We will discuss the forthcoming challenges and directions of research in this field. This review aims at giving an overview of the practical value and constraints of AI in the educational sector so that educators can have a clearer idea of what can and cannot be achieved by AI, and at the same time, helping the AI researchers to realize the specifics of pedagogical knowledge and technical features in an educational system.
